text:
Agile methodology is a way of managing projects, often used in software development. Instead of planning everything at once, work is divided into short phases called sprints. Each sprint usually lasts two to four weeks.

Daily standup meetings help the team stay on track. Everyone briefly answers three questions: what they did yesterday, what they’ll do today, and what problems they face. The team uses a product backlog, which is a list of all the features needed. Each feature is described as a user story, like “As a user, I want to reset my password.”

Agile is based on iterative development. After each sprint, the team reviews the work and adjusts plans. This way, the product improves steadily, and changes are easier to handle.
